agents.summary: Owns overall control-plane behavior, run transitions, policy loading, and pause/resume routing.
Checks ticket or user input sufficiency before planning proceeds.
Classifies work into feature, enhancement, bug fix, or maintenance flows.
Determines safe autonomy level based on ambiguity, risk, and blast radius.
Maps the request to existing capabilities, touched areas, and likely shared dependencies.
Owns lightweight asset create/revise/version-update/remove flows for sync-managed or repo-owned capability assets.
Checks branch-local and shared memory state for in-flight overlapping work.
Produces the canonical plan of action and keeps it aligned with intake, risk, and capability decisions.
Performs architecture compliance review against repo design principles and capability-first rules.
Reviews command UX, DX, contributor ergonomics, and operator clarity.
Applies code and artifact changes after the planning and review loops are clear.
Designs difficult, acceptance-linked validation scenarios tailored to the change.
Protects already-working behavior by selecting and interpreting touched-scope regression tests.
Designs and authors new tests reflecting changed or newly introduced behavior.
Executes the authored and selected test plan, respecting pipx and local-resource locks.
Installs and validates the actual packaged artifact rather than stopping at dev-mode checks.
Confirms intent alignment, regression posture, and release readiness before prerelease/PR progression.
Updates branch release notes, creates the prerelease, and assembles stable-note context when needed.
Downloads and validates the actual published prerelease artifact for the current host before PR progression.
Creates the PR and updates external systems such as Jira through the frozen integration paths.
Preserves prompts, decisions, iteration counts, and resume state so runs stay auditable and resumable.
